Best Tour Packages for Rajasthan from India – Discover the Royal Land of Kings

If you're planning your next adventure, the best tour packages for Rajasthan from India offer an unforgettable journey through royal forts, colorful cities, and timeless traditions. Recommended by best domestic tour operators in India and loved by thousands of Indian travelers, these packages ensure a seamless and memorable exploration of Rajasthan’s charm.

Why Choose Rajasthan for Your Next Trip?

Majestic Palaces and Forts
Rajasthan is home to some of India’s most iconic architectural marvels, including the UNESCO-listed Amber Fort, Mehrangarh Fort, and City Palace. These historic structures tell stories of valor, luxury, and ancient craftsmanship that captivate history buffs and photographers alike.

Rich Culture, Festivals, and Heritage
Every corner of Rajasthan bursts with colors, from the vibrant Holi and Diwali celebrations to iconic fairs like the Pushkar Camel Fair. Visitors can also participate in traditional workshops like block printing, puppet-making, or Rajasthani cooking to truly immerse themselves in the cultural experience.

Perfect for History Buffs, Families, and Couples
Whether you're a solo traveler seeking heritage, a couple planning a romantic escape, or a group on a Rajasthan family tour package, the state has something for every traveler. Explore the narrow lanes of Jodhpur, go boating in Udaipur, or ride camels in Jaisalmer—each tailored package creates lasting memories.

Top Rajasthan Tour Packages from India

5 Days Rajasthan Tour Package from India – Highlights of Jaipur, Jodhpur & Udaipur
Explore three iconic cities in just five days with guided tours, palace visits, and comfortable transport.

Sample Itinerary:
Day 1: Jaipur Arrival + City Palace Tour
Day 2: Amber Fort + Drive to Jodhpur
Day 3: Mehrangarh Fort + Drive to Udaipur
Day 4: Udaipur Lake Tour + Cultural Show
Day 5: Departure

Rajasthan Heritage Tour Packages from India
Experience timeless charm as you stay in heritage properties like Neemrana Fort Palace or Samode Haveli. These packages are ideal for travelers who want to relive royal lifestyles and explore history through architecture and storytelling.

Budget-Friendly Rajasthan Tour Packages for Indian Travelers
Discover Rajasthan on a budget with Rajasthan tour packages under 10000 that cover essential sights, local transport, and cozy stays. Traveling in off-peak seasons or booking in groups can unlock even more savings.

Customized Rajasthan Tour Packages for Families and Groups
Enjoy the flexibility of personalizing your journey—add camel safaris, cultural dinners, or child-friendly sightseeing. Custom Rajasthan family tour package options are available from all major cities and include private transport, flexible meal plans, and activity add-ons.

Must-Visit Destinations Covered in Rajasthan Tour Packages

Jaipur – The Pink City
Famed for Hawa Mahal, City Palace, and Amer Fort, Jaipur also offers authentic shopping at Johari Bazaar and traditional meals at Chokhi Dhani.

Udaipur – The City of Lakes
Known for its romantic vibe, Udaipur is a favorite in budget-friendly Rajasthan honeymoon packages. Enjoy boat rides on Lake Pichola and dinner at rooftop restaurants overlooking City Palace.

Jodhpur – The Blue City
The iconic Mehrangarh Fort and blue-washed alleys make Jodhpur a visual delight. Don’t miss a meal at a rooftop café with a fort view, or a visit to the bustling Sardar Market.

Jaisalmer – The Golden City and Desert Safari
Camp under the stars at Sam Sand Dunes, ride camels across golden landscapes, and enjoy cultural folk performances in the Thar Desert.

Pushkar – Famous for the Holy Lake and Camel Fair
Visit the sacred Brahma Temple, take a holy dip in the Pushkar Lake, and plan your trip during Kartik Purnima to witness the famous camel fair.

What’s Included in the Best Rajasthan Tour Packages from India?
​
Comfortable Accommodation and Meals
Options include stays in heritage hotels, boutique resorts, and budget lodgings, with veg, non-veg, and Jain food options available across most packages.

Airport Transfers and Local Transport
AC coaches or private vehicles are included for intercity travel. Always confirm the type of transport and check for group or private options when booking.

Guided Sightseeing and Cultural Experiences
Certified local guides provide immersive historical insights and help you connect deeply with Rajasthan’s stories and traditions.

Best Time to Visit Rajasthan from India

Ideal Seasons for Rajasthan Travel – Winter and Monsoon

Visit from October to March for the best weather, or consider monsoon months (July–September) for greener landscapes and fewer crowds.

Season                         Benefits
Winter               Ideal climate, peak tourist season
Monsoon           Budget prices, offbeat charm
Summer            Hot but ideal for desert photography

Tips to Avoid Crowds and Get the Best Deals

Traveling on weekdays, booking early, and exploring less crowded destinations like Bundi or Bikaner can help you find affordable Rajasthan tour packages for families with great value.

Frequently Asked Questions About Rajasthan Tour Packages from India

How long should a Rajasthan trip last?
7 to 10 days is ideal to explore major cities and unique experiences. For shorter vacations, try Jaipur + Pushkar in 3 days or a quick Jaisalmer desert weekend.

Are Rajasthan tours safe for families and solo travelers?
Yes, Rajasthan is one of India’s most tourist-friendly states. Many operators provide affordable Rajasthan tour packages for families and best value Rajasthan tour packages for solo travelers with safe accommodations, 24/7 support, and guided assistance.

Can Rajasthan packages be customized to suit my interests?
Absolutely! Whether you choose Rajasthan tour packages from Pune, Mumbai, Kolkata, or Delhi, customization options include food preferences, accommodation upgrades, and activities like cooking classes or rural village walks.

What are the best options for honeymooners?
Couples can enjoy budget-friendly Rajasthan honeymoon packages featuring spa treatments, candlelight dinners, and romantic sunset boat rides in Udaipur. These are also popular among travelers seeking honeymoon packages from Kolkata and other major cities.

What if I’m traveling alone—are there solo-friendly packages?
Yes, solo travelers can opt for best value Rajasthan tour packages for solo travelers that include small group tours, cultural activities, and hostels or homestays with strong community vibes.

How to Book the Best Rajasthan Tour Packages from India

Trusted Travel Agencies and Online Booking Platforms

Book through trusted platforms like TravelXploria, MakeMyTrip, and Yatra. These companies are often ranked among the best tour and travel company in India, known for transparent pricing, verified itineraries, and great customer service. If you're searching for a travel agency in Kolkata near me, these platforms also have strong networks in West Bengal.

Important Tips Before Booking Your Rajasthan Tour

Always verify hotel ratings, check if transportation is private or shared, and confirm if inclusions cover monument entry fees. Look out for Rajasthan tour packages under 10000 for short but fulfilling escapes.

Essential Documents and Packing Advice

Carry government-issued ID (Aadhar or PAN), booking receipts, and essential medications. Pack cotton clothes, a warm layer for cool desert nights, sunscreen, hats, and sturdy walking shoes. International travelers should carry passports and a copy of their visa.

packages.overview:
• Birla Temple: Marvel at the serene architecture of this stunning temple, dedicated to Lord Vishnu and Lakshmi.

  • Jaigarh Fort: Explore the grandeur of this historic fort, offering panoramic views of Jaipur and its surroundings.

  • Sisodia Rani Ka Bagh: Stroll through the lush gardens with intricate frescoes, providing a peaceful retreat from city life.

  • Choki Dhani: Immerse yourself in authentic Rajasthani culture with traditional performances and a delightful Rajasthani feast.

  • Jantar Mantar: Discover the fascinating astronomical instruments at this UNESCO World Heritage Site, offering insight into ancient science.

  • Nahargarh Fort: Enjoy breathtaking views of Jaipur from this majestic hilltop fort, blending history and scenic beauty.

  • Amer Fort: Explore the regal charm of this grand fort, showcasing rich Rajasthani architecture and history.

  • Hawa Mahal: Admire the iconic pink façade and intricate lattice work of this palace, a symbol of Jaipur's royal heritage.

  • Johri Bazaar: Wander through the bustling market, perfect for souvenir hunting, especially for traditional jewelry.

  • Shri Laxminath Temple: Visit this tranquil and historic temple, dedicated to Lord Vishnu and Lakshmi.

  • Karni Mata Temple: Experience the unique and sacred site, famous for its thousands of holy rats.

  • Jaisalmer Fort (Sonar Kella): Explore the golden grandeur of Jaisalmer’s fort, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

  • Patwon-Ki-Haveli: Step into this architectural marvel, showcasing intricate carvings and ancient grandeur.

  • Sam Sand Dunes: Feel the thrill of a camel safari across the vast golden desert, where the beauty of Rajasthan unfolds.

  • Desert National Park: Explore the unique ecosystem of the desert, home to rare wildlife and stunning landscapes.

  • Mehrangarh Fort: Discover the historical richness of Jodhpur’s imposing fort, offering spectacular views of the “Blue City.”

  • Umaid Bhavan Palace: Visit this majestic palace, a blend of royal history and luxurious heritage.

  • Jodhpur Markets: Shop for authentic Jodhpuri footwear, a perfect souvenir to remember your journey.

packages.overview:
1. Rajasthan: A royal state rich in heritage, palaces, desert charm, and wildlife adventures.

  2. Mount Abu: The only hill station in Rajasthan, known for its cool climate, Nakki Lake, Dilwara Temples, and sunset points.

  3. Udaipur: The romantic “City of Lakes,” offering Lake Pichola, City Palace, and traditional Rajasthani culture.

  4. Pushkar: A spiritual town famed for the sacred Pushkar Lake, Brahma Temple, and vibrant camel fair.

  5. Ranthambore: A haven for wildlife lovers, known for tiger safaris and ancient Ranthambore Fort within the national park.

  6. Jaipur: The Pink City, rich with historic forts, Hawa Mahal, City Palace, and colorful bazaars.

On the way, stop by the picturesque Jal Mahal, a stunning palace floating in the middle of Man Sagar Lake. After lunch, continue the journey with visits to the Nahargarh Fort and Jaigarh Fort, offering breathtaking views of the city, followed by the sacred Galta Ji Temple, known for its natural water springs. As the evening sets in, immerse yourself in the vibrant cultural experience at Chokhi Dhani, a traditional Rajasthani village. After an eventful day, return to the hotel for a relaxing overnight stay.

packages.package_name: Rajasthan Highlights A Tale of Kings, Lakes & Legends
packages.overview:
• Statue Circle & Birla Planetarium – Discover Jaipur’s modern charm with architectural marvels and celestial wonders.

  • Birla Mandir & Vidhan Sabha – Admire the pristine temple and the grand legislative building.

  • Hawa Mahal & City Palace – Witness the regal elegance and intricate craftsmanship of Jaipur’s heritage.

  • Jantar Mantar & Jal Mahal – Experience astronomical brilliance and the serene beauty of a palace floating on water.

  • Amer Fort & Nahargarh Fort – Relive Rajasthan’s glorious past through majestic forts and panoramic city views.

  • Ajmer Sharif Dargah & Brahma Temple – Embrace spiritual serenity at two of India’s most revered pilgrimage sites.

  • City Palace & Saheliyon Ki Bari – Walk through Udaipur’s royal grandeur and lush historic gardens.

  • Bagore Ki Haveli & Fateh Sagar Lake – Experience cultural richness and unwind by Udaipur’s picturesque lake.

  • Shilp Gram & Bhartiya Lok Kala Mandal – Immerse in Rajasthan’s folk art, handicrafts, and traditional performances.

packages.package_name: A Royal Journey Through Rajasthan’s Landmarks
packages.overview:
1. Rajasthan: A vibrant land of royalty, rich culture, desert landscapes, and magnificent architecture.

  2. Jaipur: The Pink City, famed for Amber Fort, Hawa Mahal, City Palace, and colorful bazaars full of handicrafts and jewels.

  3. Udaipur: The romantic City of Lakes, offering serene boat rides on Lake Pichola, the majestic City Palace, and vibrant cultural performances.

  4. Jodhpur: The Blue City, home to the imposing Mehrangarh Fort, Umaid Bhawan Palace, and bustling local markets.

  5. Jaisalmer: The Golden City of the Thar Desert, known for Sonar Quila (Jaisalmer Fort), desert safaris, and sand dune camping.

  6. Bikaner: Renowned for its Junagarh Fort, camel breeding farm, and Karni Mata Temple, adding a unique desert charm.

After breakfast, bid farewell to Jaisalmer and set off for Bikaner, a city renowned for its rich heritage and royal charm. Upon arrival, visit the magnificent Junagarh Fort, an architectural marvel with grand palaces, intricate carvings, and fascinating museums. Next, explore the sacred Karni Mata Temple, famously known as the \"Rat Temple,\" where thousands of revered rats reside. packages.overview:
• Rajasthan: A royal land of majestic forts, vibrant culture, golden deserts, and rich wildlife experiences.

  • Jaipur: The Pink City enchants with Amber Fort, Hawa Mahal, City Palace, and lively local bazaars filled with textiles and handicrafts.

  • Jodhpur: The Blue City is known for the grand Mehrangarh Fort, Jaswant Thada, and its charming old town filled with indigo houses.

  • Jaisalmer: The Golden City offers a magical desert experience with Jaisalmer Fort, Sam Sand Dunes, and camel safaris under starlit skies.

  1. Udaipur: The romantic City of Lakes, known for Lake Pichola, City Palace, Jag Mandir, and picturesque sunsets.

  • Ranthambore: A thrilling stop for wildlife lovers, offering jeep safaris through the national park to spot tigers, leopards, and more.

packages.overview:
• Sonar Quila – Explore India’s only living fort, a golden-hued marvel where history breathes through its vibrant alleys and ancient Havelis.

  • Patwon ki Haveli – Step into a 500-year-old architectural masterpiece, once home to Jaisalmer’s wealthiest merchants, adorned with intricate carvings.

  • Jain Temples – Discover exquisitely sculpted temples showcasing Jaisalmer’s deep spiritual roots and the grandeur of Jain architecture.

  • Sam Sand Dunes – Embark on a thrilling desert adventure with camel safaris, jeep rides, and breathtaking sunset views.

  • Cultural Night – Experience Rajasthan’s vibrant spirit with mesmerizing folk performances and a lavish traditional dinner under the star-lit sky.

  • Tanot Mata Temple – Witness the legend of a temple that miraculously withstood enemy shelling during the Indo-Pak war.

  • Longewala Post – Relive history at the battleground of the 1971 war, where valor and patriotism echo through every corner.

  • Jaisalmer War Museum – Pay tribute to India’s brave soldiers while exploring war relics, inspiring stories, and battle history.

  • Tazia Tower – Admire this five-story architectural marvel, reflecting a blend of Rajput and Mughal craftsmanship.

packages.overview:
• City Palace – A stunning fusion of Rajput and Mughal architecture, showcasing royal grandeur and intricate artistry.

  • Hawa Mahal – The iconic Palace of Winds, glowing in pink hues, offering a mesmerizing evening spectacle.

  • Amber Fort – A majestic fortress with ornate mirror work and breathtaking carvings, best explored on a royal elephant ride.

  • Jaigarh Fort – Home to grand cannons and panoramic Aravalli views, whispering tales of valor.

  • Jal Mahal – A floating architectural gem, reflecting Jaipur’s regal charm in the serene Man Sagar Lake.

  • Chittorgarh Fort – India’s largest fort, echoing legendary tales of Rani Padmini’s valiant spirit.

  • Lake Pichola – Udaipur’s enchanting waters, perfect for a romantic boat ride with a mesmerizing sunset.

  • City Palace Udaipur – A majestic structure with spellbinding views of the lake and the city’s timeless beauty.

  • Saheliyon Ki Bari – A serene garden retreat adorned with delicate fountains, marble pavilions, and lotus-filled pools.

  • Ranakpur Jain Temple – A marvel of exquisite marble carvings and divine serenity, hidden amidst lush Aravalli hills.

  • Mehrangarh Fort – Jodhpur’s towering guardian, offering breathtaking views and showcasing royal heritage.

  • Jaswant Thada – An ethereal marble cenotaph, radiating peace and Rajput grandeur.

  • Jaisalmer Fort – A living, golden fortress with narrow alleys echoing medieval charm.

  • Salim Singh ki Haveli – A fascinating architectural wonder with an arched roof resembling a peacock.

  • Patwon-Ki-Haveli – An elaborate cluster of havelis showcasing intricate facades and grand balconies.

  • Nathmal Ji Ki Haveli – A masterpiece of craftsmanship, blending Rajput and Mughal influences.

  • Gadisar Lake – A tranquil oasis where history meets serenity under Jaisalmer’s golden skies.

  • Thar Desert Camel Safari – A magical journey across rolling dunes, culminating in a cultural spectacle under starlit skies.

packages.overview:
• Hawa Mahal – Witness the grandeur of Jaipur’s iconic "Palace of Winds," a stunning architectural marvel with intricate latticework.

  • Birla Temple – Experience divine serenity at this beautifully crafted white marble temple.

  1. Sisodiya Rani Bagh – Stroll through lush gardens with cascading fountains and intricate murals narrating royal love stories.

  • Jal Mahal – Admire the ethereal beauty of this floating palace on the tranquil waters of Man Sagar Lake.

  • Jantar Mantar – Marvel at the astronomical precision of this UNESCO-listed observatory.

  • Nahargarh Fort – Capture breathtaking panoramic views of Jaipur’s skyline from this historic hilltop fortress.

  • Abhaneri Step Well – Be amazed by the stunning geometric patterns of this ancient water reservoir.

  • Chokhi Dhani – Immerse in Rajasthani culture with vibrant folk performances and a delectable traditional feast.

  • Sambhar Lake – Witness the vast, glistening saltwater expanse, home to mesmerizing flocks of flamingos.

  • Pushkar Lake – Feel the divine aura of this sacred lake, surrounded by 52 picturesque ghats.

  • Brahma Temple – Pay homage at one of the world’s rarest temples dedicated to Lord Brahma.

  • Savitri Mata Temple – Hike up to this serene hilltop temple for mesmerizing panoramic views of Pushkar.

  • Ajmer Dargah – Seek blessings at the revered shrine of Sufi saint Khwaja Moinuddin Chisti.

  • Ranthambore National Park – Embark on a thrilling safari to spot majestic tigers in their natural habitat.

  • Ranthambore Fort – Step into history as you explore this ancient fort amidst the wilderness.

  • Khandar Fort – Discover the rustic charm of this lesser-known yet fascinating fort.

packages.overview:
1. Rajasthan: India’s royal state, known for its grand palaces, desert landscapes, and rich cultural heritage.

  2. Jaipur: The Pink City, famous for Amer Fort, City Palace, Hawa Mahal, and vibrant bazaars.

  3. Udaipur: The City of Lakes, featuring the stunning Lake Pichola, City Palace, and Jag Mandir, ideal for romantic getaways.

  4. Mount Abu: Rajasthan’s only hill station, offering cool weather, the serene Nakki Lake, and the intricate Dilwara Temples.

Known as the only hill station in Rajasthan, Mount Abu offers a refreshing escape, with temperatures sometimes dropping to zero degrees during winter. Upon arrival, check into the hotel and relax. packages.overview:
Rajasthan: A royal state in India known for its majestic forts, desert landscapes, and vibrant culture.

Jaipur: The Pink City, home to Amer Fort, Hawa Mahal, City Palace, and bustling markets.

Bikaner: Famous for Junagarh Fort, Karni Mata Temple (Rat Temple), and delicious Bikaneri snacks.

Jaisalmer: The Golden City, known for Jaisalmer Fort, Sam Sand Dunes, and thrilling desert safaris.

Jodhpur: The Blue City, featuring Mehrangarh Fort, Umaid Bhawan Palace, and vibrant blue-hued streets.

Udaipur: The City of Lakes, offering romantic boat rides on Lake Pichola and stunning palaces.

Mount Abu: Rajasthan’s only hill station, known for Nakki Lake and the intricate Dilwara Temples.

packages.overview:
1. Rajasthan: A land of majestic forts, serene lakes, and timeless heritage that captures the essence of royal India.

  2. Udaipur: Known as the “City of Lakes,” Udaipur enchants with Lake Pichola, City Palace, Jag Mandir, and romantic boat rides amidst stunning architecture.

  3. Mount Abu: Rajasthan’s only hill station offers a cool escape with Nakki Lake, Dilwara Temples, sunset points, and lush greenery.

  4. Kumbhalgarh: Famous for its massive fortress wall (second longest in the world) and scenic surroundings, ideal for history and nature lovers alike.

After a delightful breakfast, embark on a journey to explore the charm of Udaipur, famously known as the City of Lakes. Begin with a visit to the majestic City Palace, a symbol of grandeur and rich heritage. Seek blessings at the historic Jagdish Temple before discovering the vibrant culture at Bhartiya Lok Kala Mandal. Pay homage to the legendary Maharana Pratap at Pratap Smarak and explore the artistic village of Shilp Gram. Marvel at the intricate architecture of Bagore ki Haveli and stroll through the serene gardens of Saheliyon ki Bari. Overnight stay at your hotel in Udaipur.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Udaipur Sightseeing with HaldiGhati & Nathdwara ","day_summary":"

Visit the legendary Haldighati, the battlefield where Maharana Pratap and his army displayed immense valor. Explore the Haldighati Museum, which beautifully showcases the region's glorious past. After an awe-inspiring day, return to the hotel, unwind, and savor an authentic Rajasthani dinner before calling it a night.

packages.overview:
• Rajasthan: A majestic land of heritage, desert charm, and vibrant traditions.

  • Jaisalmer: Known as the Golden City, it captivates with the stunning Jaisalmer Fort (Sonar Quila), ornate havelis, and thrilling desert safaris at Sam Sand Dunes. Enjoy camel rides, cultural performances, and camping under the stars.

  • Jodhpur: The Blue City offers a royal experience with the mighty Mehrangarh Fort, serene Jaswant Thada, and bustling bazaars filled with spices, textiles, and handicrafts.

En route to your desert safari, embark on an unforgettable camel-back or jeep ride across the golden dunes of Sam and Khuri with your newly wedded wife. A stop at the Akal Wood Fossil Park offers a glimpse into ancient geological wonders. For thrill-seekers, paragliding over the desert is a must-try adventure. After lunch, explore the Desert National Park, home to unique wildlife. As the sun sets, immerse yourself in folk music and dance performances at a vibrant desert camp, followed by a traditional Rajasthani dinner. Unwind in your cozy luxury tent, soaking in the magic of the desert night.

Return to the hotel for a delightful farewell lunch before checking out. With hearts full of memories and phones brimming with stunning captures, bid adieu to Rajasthan’s royal charm as you head towards Jodhpur for your flight or train home.

overview:
Season
Benefits


Best Tour Packages for Rajasthan from India – Discover the Royal Land of Kings

If you're planning your next adventure, the best tour packages for Rajasthan from India offer an unforgettable journey through royal forts, colorful cities, and timeless traditions. Recommended by best domestic tour operators in India and loved by thousands of Indian travelers, these packages ensure a seamless and memorable exploration of Rajasthan’s charm.

Why Choose Rajasthan for Your Next Trip?

Majestic Palaces and Forts
Rajasthan is home to some of India’s most iconic architectural marvels, including the UNESCO-listed Amber Fort, Mehrangarh Fort, and City Palace. These historic structures tell stories of valor, luxury, and ancient craftsmanship that captivate history buffs and photographers alike.

Rich Culture, Festivals, and Heritage
Every corner of Rajasthan bursts with colors, from the vibrant Holi and Diwali celebrations to iconic fairs like the Pushkar Camel Fair. Visitors can also participate in traditional workshops like block printing, puppet-making, or Rajasthani cooking to truly immerse themselves in the cultural experience.

Perfect for History Buffs, Families, and Couples
Whether you're a solo traveler seeking heritage, a couple planning a romantic escape, or a group on a Rajasthan family tour package, the state has something for every traveler. Explore the narrow lanes of Jodhpur, go boating in Udaipur, or ride camels in Jaisalmer—each tailored package creates lasting memories.

Top Rajasthan Tour Packages from India

5 Days Rajasthan Tour Package from India – Highlights of Jaipur, Jodhpur & Udaipur
Explore three iconic cities in just five days with guided tours, palace visits, and comfortable transport.

Sample Itinerary:
Day 1: Jaipur Arrival + City Palace Tour
Day 2: Amber Fort + Drive to Jodhpur
Day 3: Mehrangarh Fort + Drive to Udaipur
Day 4: Udaipur Lake Tour + Cultural Show
Day 5: Departure

Rajasthan Heritage Tour Packages from India
Experience timeless charm as you stay in heritage properties like Neemrana Fort Palace or Samode Haveli. These packages are ideal for travelers who want to relive royal lifestyles and explore history through architecture and storytelling.

Budget-Friendly Rajasthan Tour Packages for Indian Travelers
